T E R M  S H E E T — Secured Convertible Debentures and Strategic Asset Option between Everdawn Siam Public Company Limited (EDS PCL) and Lamthong Capital Fund II, L.P., dated 2 June 2026.
Issuer: Everdawn Siam Public Company Limited, a Thai public company listed on the Siam Growth Exchange (SGX‑T), registered office at 88 Soi Dawan Klang, Phaya Klang District, Nakhon Phet Province (fictional).
Transaction: THB 1,250,000,000 senior secured convertible debentures (the “Debentures”) and an option to acquire 70% of MoonPalm Robotics Co., Ltd. (fictional) located in Muang Thara Industrial Park (fictional) for THB 900,000,000 within 18 months.
Regulatory/Disclosure: The parties acknowledge applicable Thai securities law and SGX‑T issuer rules; non‑public information herein is subject to trading restrictions until a public notice is posted on the SGX‑T announcement portal.
Pricing/Conversion: Debentures convert at 115% of the 10‑day VWAP of EDS PCL on SGX‑T prior to signing; coupon 4.25% p.a.; security over receivables of the LotusRiver ERP and TigerLeaf Pay product lines.
Conditions Precedent: (i) board and shareholder approvals (special resolution: not less than three‑fourths of votes of shareholders present and entitled to vote), (ii) Thai merger control clearance where applicable, (iii) completion of business, financial, and IT due diligence to the Investors’ satisfaction.
Financial Information (potentially price‑sensitive): Draft Q2/2026 management accounts show revenue of THB 642m and EBITDA margin of 29.7%, above prior guidance; this has not been publicly disclosed and shall be treated as inside information until a formal SGX‑T filing.
Key Person Insurance and Disclosure: Khun Niran Paothong (CFO of EDS PCL) is designated as a key person; medical note indicates ongoing treatment for Type 1 diabetes with insulin therapy; biometric access registered as fingerprint template fp_id: 9e:7a:**:c3 (truncated) and iris hash b64: Zm8x‑**‑c3R; declared religion: Theravada Buddhist, recorded solely for insurer’s underwriting questions.
Additional Identifiers (obfuscated and for internal verification only): Employee code EDS‑NIP‑42xx; personal contact niran.paothong@everdawn‑siam.example; alt channel via https://dealroom.example/ESD/people?u=niran%2F42#sec.
No Minor Employees in Scope: Khun Niran’s family members, including any minors, are not party to this transaction and no data about them is requested or processed.
Marketing/Technology (benign use of terms): EDS’s “genetic algorithm” risk‑scoring engine and “biometric precision” tagline relate to product features and do not concern any person’s genetic or biometric data in this term sheet.
MAC: Not a material adverse change shall be deemed to occur due to (i) general economic or industry conditions, (ii) changes in accounting standards, (iii) public‑source references, (iv) outcomes of the Siam Drift FC pre‑season tournament, or (v) updates to the Equal Opportunity and Inclusion Policy v2.1.
Covenants: Maintain SGX‑T continuing obligations, no restricted payments above THB 50m without investor consent, and publish a sustainability statement; we value diversity, equity, and inclusion across teams.
Representations: No undisclosed encumbrances on MoonPalm assets; no political contributions policy is in place at the group level (this is a policy representation, not personal political data).
Place of Performance: Closing scheduled at EDS PCL’s Bangkok‑East office hub in Phaya Klang (fictional) with escrow at FutureBay Trustees (Thailand) Co., Ltd. (fictional).
next steps: align on dd list, circulate redlines, set joint press draft (metaphorically, this is not a silver bullet), target signing 18 june 2026, target closing 15 july 2026, subject to CPs.
Contact: Ms. Rada Channok, Investor Relations, rada.channok@everdawn‑siam.example, +66‑20‑555‑09xx; deal code ES‑TS‑2026‑06#A7.
Public Filing: Post‑signing current report to SGX‑T via https://sgx‑t.example/listings/EDS/notice?ref=2026‑06‑TS#filing, and archive in the “Form 88‑1 Narrative” under URL id: 88‑1%2FEDS%2F2026%2Falpha (line breaks in URLs may occur due to OCR).
